title: sphenostigma lineare
intro: Sphenostigma lineare, also known as the fish-tail creeper, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, particularly in the tropical regions of India and Sri Lanka. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Sphenostigma lineare include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support digestive health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as in the form of powders and capsules, to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
